qt: Which of the following statements about open balls in a metric space is true?
op: An open ball always includes its boundary points.
The centre of an open ball is not necessarily contained in the ball.
Every open ball is an open set in the metric space.
The radius of an open ball can be negative.

qt: A homoemorphic map between two topological spaces is always …………..
Two spaces are called topologically equivalent if there exists a ………… between two topological spaces.
A topological property is a property of a topological space that is preserved under homeomorphisms, which of the following represent the topological property ..........
$${T_{X\, \times \,Y}}$$ denotes ...........on $X\, \times \,Y$.
Which of the following best describes a projection map in topology?
op: A continuous map from a topological space to a discrete set.
A map that assigns every point in a Cartesian product space to its first component.
A homeomorphism between two topological spaces.
A map that preserves the open sets of a topological space.

qt: A topological space is said to be the second countable space if it has a ..............base.
A map ‘f’ from a topological space X to a topological space Y is ………..if and only if the image of each closed subset of X is closed  in Y.
A map ‘f’ between two topological spaces is called …………… iff ‘f’ is bijective, continuous and f-1(f inverse) is continuous.
Every metric space is first countable.
Let $X$ and $Y$ be topological spaces. A map $f:X \to Y$ is called a Closed Map if________
op: for every open set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is open.
for every closed set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is closed
for every open set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is closed.

qt: A topological space is said to be a separable space if it has a ................subset.
Which of the following properties must a relation R on a set A satisfy to be an equivalence relation?
$${\text{Metric topology induced by }}d(x,y) = |x - y{\text{| on }}\mathbb{R}{\text{ is called\_\_\_\_\_}}$$
A first countable space is a topological space in which there exist a..................... base at each of its point.
Let $X$ and $Y$ be topological spaces. A map $f:X \to Y$ is called an Open Map if________
op: for every open set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is open.
for every closed set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is closed.
for every open set $U \subseteq X$, the image $f(U) \subseteq Y$ is closed.

qt: Let X and Y be two topological spaces. If f is a function from X to Y, then f is said to be homeomorphism if..............
A topological space (X,ͳ) is said to be metrizable if there is a metric d on X that generate.... ...
Let$X = R$(Set of real numbers) be a usual metric space and $N \subseteq R$, then which of the following is NOT true about $N$ ?
op: It is not a neighbourhood of any of its point.
Neither of its point is an interior point.
It must be an open set.

qt: A topological space X is said to be separable if it has a countable dense subset. Which of the following is a countable dense subset of the real numbers?
op: The set of all real numbers

qt: Which of the following statements best defines a metric space?
op: A set that is equipped with a topology.
A set where every element has a unique inverse element.
A set with a binary operation that combines any two elements to form a third element.
A set with a function that measures the distance between any two elements in the set.

qt: If $(X,d)$ is a compact metric space, then______
op: every sequence which has a convergent subsequence is a Cauchy sequence.
every sequence is a Cauchy sequence.
every sequence has a subsequence which is a Cauchy sequence.

qt: Let$X = R$(Set of real numbers) be a usual metric space and $N \subseteq R$, then which of the following is true about $N$ ?
op: It is not an open set.
None of its point is an interior point.
It has disjoint intersection with the set of its interior points.

qt: What is an open cover of a topological space X?
op: A collection of open sets that are disjoint.
A single open set that contains X.
A collection of closed sets whose intersection is X.
A collection of open sets whose union is X.

qt: If $X$ has more than two points and $\left( {X,\tau } \right)$ be an indiscrete topology then which of the following statement is true about $\left( {X,\tau } \right)$ ?
Let $X = \left\{ {1,2,3,4,5,6} \right\}$ and $\tau = \left\{ {\emptyset ,\{ 1\} ,\{ 2\} ,\{ 1,2\} ,X} \right\}$ be a topology on $X$, then the local base ( ${B_x}$ ) of the point $x = 1$ is_______
Let $X = \left\{ {1,2,3,4} \right\}$ and $\tau = \left\{ {\emptyset ,\{ 1\} ,\{ 2\} ,\{ 1,2\} ,X} \right\}$ be a topology on $X$, then which of the following is NOT true ?
op: Every element of $X$ has uncountable local base.
The local base of the element 4 is $\emptyset$.

qt: Which of the following statements correctly characterizes a T1 topological space?
Given a Hausdorff space $(X, T)$ and a subset $Y \subset X$, with the subspace topology $(Y, T_{Y})$, which of the following statements is true?
op: Y with the subspace topology $(Y, T_{Y})$ is also Hausdorff.
Y with the subspace topology $(Y, T_{Y})$ is not Hausdorff.

qt: Which of the following is an example of a metric space that is also Hausdorff?
op: The discrete metric space on any set.
The set of complex numbers $\mathbb{C}$ with the metric defined on the set of complex numbers.
The set of real numbers $\mathbb{R}$ with the standard Euclidean metric.

qt: Which of the following statements is true about discrete topological space?
op: Every discrete topological space is regular.
Every discrete topological space may or may not be regular.
Every discrete topological space is not regular.

qt: Which of the following space is a Housdorff?
op: A non empty set X with indiscrete space.
An Infinite set with cofinite toplogy.
Real numbers with toplogy generated by { ${{(a, \infty) | a \in R}}$}
Real numbers with Lower limit topology.

qt: Which of the following statements is equivalent to the statement that a topological space X is T1?
op: For any two distinct points x and y in X, there exists an open set V such that y ∈ V and x ∉ V.
For any two distinct points x and y in X, there exist disjoint open sets U and V such that x ∈ U and y ∈ V.
For any two distinct points x and y in X, there exists an open set U such that x ∈ U and y ∉ U.

qt: Let X be a topological space. Which of the following statement is true?
op: Every pair of disjoint closed sets in X can be separated by closed sets.
Every pair of disjoint closed sets in X can be separated by open sets.

qt: Which of the following statements is equivalent to the statement that a topological space X is regular?
op: For any point x in X and any closed set F such that x ∉ F, there exists a closed set G such that x ∈ G and F ∩ G = ∅.
A topological space X is said to be regular if, for every point x in X and every closed set F in X such that x ∉ F, there exist disjoint open sets U and V in X such that x ∈ U and F ⊆ V.

qt: Which of the following statements is true regarding finite T1​ spaces?
op: Every finite T1​ space is discrete.
Every finite T1​ ​ space is T3 .​
Every finite T1​ space is indiscrete.

qt: A topological space is a $T_{1}$- space if and only if each of its finite subset is ....
Which of the following topological spaces is Hausdorff?
op: The set of all real numbers with the usual topology.
The set of all infinite sets with the discrete topology.
The set of all points in the plane with the Euclidean topology.
The set of all finite sets with the discrete topology.

qt: Which of the following statements is true about Hausdorff spaces?
op: Every sequence has a convergent subsequence.
Every pair of distinct points can be separated by overlapping open sets.
Every pair of distinct points can be separated by disjoint open sets.

qt: Which of the following statements is true about metric spaces?
op: Every metric space is not Hausdorff.

qt: If $\left( {X,\tau } \right)$ be a separable topology then it must have countable dense set.
Which of the following is a correct reason why every subspace of a Hausdorff space is also Hausdorff?
op: Because the subspace have different separation properties from the parent space.
Because every subspace is closed in a Hausdorff space.
Because every subspace of a topological space is always Hausdorff.
Because the subspace inherits the separation properties from the parent space.

qt: Which of the following statements about an indiscrete topological space is true?
Which of the following is not an example of compact spaces?
op: A finite set with any toplogy.
An infinite set with discrete topology.
A set with topology containing finite number of elements.

qt: Which of the following statements correctly compares T1​ and T0 properties?
op: T1​ property implies T0 property, but not vice versa.
T0​ property is a stronger property than T1​ property.
T0​ property implies T1​ property, but not vice versa.
T1​ property is equivalent to T0 property.

qt: Consider a set $X$ with the indiscrete topology. What can be said about the singleton sets in $X$?
op: They are neither open nor closed.
They are closed but not open.
They are open but not closed.

qt: If (X,d) is a compact metric space, then______
op: every sequence which has a convergent subsequence is a Cauchy sequence.
every sequence has a subsequence which is a Cauchy sequence.
every sequence is a Cauchy sequence.

qt: LetX = R(Set of real numbers) be a usual metric space and N \subseteq R, then which of the following is true about N ?
op: It has disjoint intersection with the set of its interior points.
It is not an open set.
None of its point is an interior point.

qt: Let X and Y be topological spaces. A map f:X \to Y is called a Closed Map if________
op: for every open set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is open.
for every open set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is closed.
for every closed set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is closed

qt: Let X = \left\{ {1,2,3,4} \right\} and \tau = \left\{ {\emptyset ,\{ 1\} ,\{ 2\} ,\{ 1,2\} ,X} \right\} be a topology on X, then which of the following is NOT true ?

qt: Let X and Y be topological spaces. A map f:X \to Y is called an Open Map if________
op: for every open set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is closed.
for every closed set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is closed.
for every open set U \subseteq X, the image f(U) \subseteq Y is open.

qt: Let \left( {X,\tau } \right) be a metrizable then which of the following statement is true?

They are neither open nor closed.

op: Y with the subspace topology (Y, T_{Y}) is not Hausdorff.
Y with the subspace topology (Y, T_{Y}) is also Hausdorff.

qt: Given a Hausdorff space \((X, T)\) and a subset \(Y \subset X\), with the subspace topology \((Y, T_{Y})\), which of the following statements is true?
op: Y with the subspace topology \((Y, T_{Y})\) is not Hausdorff.
Y with the subspace topology \((Y, T_{Y})\) is also Hausdorff.

op: They are closed but not open.
They are neither open nor closed.
They are open but not closed.
